text: The surgeon evaluates the anatomy, identifying the base of the appendix and tracing it to the tail, highlighting the anatomical structure of the appendix.
The surgeon frees the retrocecal appendix from surrounding structures, revealing its anatomical position and allowing for further manipulation.
The tail of the appendix is freed and elevated into the surgical field, providing clear visibility for further dissection.
The surgeon creates a tunnel between the appendix and the mesoappendix, separating these structures to facilitate further dissection.
The surgeon gains access to the mesoappendix and the appendiceal artery, which are visible in the surgical field.
The surgeon uses fenestrated bipolar dissectors to coagulate the appendiceal artery, applying bipolar energy to the exposed vessel.
The surgeon uses curved scissors with a monopolar energy source to transect the freed appendix, which has been elevated into the surgical field.
The surgeon continues to free the appendix from the mesoappendix in a stepwise fashion, ensuring the blood supply is controlled throughout the process.
The tail of the appendix is elevated and freed from surrounding structures, allowing the assistant to pass a zero silk suture into the surgical field.
The surgeon ties three simple interrupted sutures around the base of the appendix, securing it after previous dissection and coagulation steps.
The surgeon uses electrocautery and sharp dissection to transect the base of the appendix, leaving a visible appendiceal stump.
The surgeon creates a purse string suture around the appendiceal stump, inverting it into the cecum.
